entries.definition:
كتاب الهمزة

أذي - أرز

(أذي) الهمزة والذال والياء أصل واحد، وهو الشيء تتكرّهُه ولا تَقَِرُّ عليه. تقول: آذَيْتُ فلاناً أُوذِيهِ. ويقال بعير أَذٍ وناقةٌ أَذِيَةٌ إذا كان لا يَقَِرّ في مكانٍ من غير وجع، وكأنه يَأْذَى بمكانه.

(باب الهمزة والراء وما معهما في الثلاثي)

that is said to him!’ [lit. he is an ear, i.e., gullible]; *(18:11) فَضَرَبْنَا
عَلَى آذَانِهِمْ We sealed their ears or We stopped them hearing [lit.
We struck on their ears].
entries.definition_english:
that is said to him!’ [lit. he is an ear, i.e., gullible]; *(18:11) فَضَرَبْنَا
عَلَى آذَانِهِمْ We sealed their ears or We stopped them hearing [lit.
We struck on their ears].

entries.definition:
ʾ–dh–y harm, injury, damage; to wrong, to harm; high
tumultuous waves. Of this root, three forms occur 24 times in the
Qur’an: آذَى ʾādhā 10 times; أُوذِيَ ʾūdhiya five times and أَذًى ʾadhan
nine times.

آذَى ʾādhā [v. IV, trans.] 1 to offend, to trouble, to
inconvenience, to malign, to affront (33:53) إِنَّ ذَلِكُمْ كَانَ يُؤْذِي النَّبِيَّ
for that inconveniences the Prophet 2 to punish, to take to task
(4:16) وَاللَّذَانِ يَأْتِيَانِهَا مِنْكُمْ فَآذُوهُمَا if any two of you commit [a lewd act]
punish them both.

أُوذِيَ ʾūdhiya [pass. of v. IV] 1 to be harmed, to be injured, to
be persecuted (6:34) وَأُوذُوا حَتَّى أَتَاهُمْ نَصْرُنَا and were persecuted
until Our aid came to them 2 to be molested, accosted, harassed,
solicited (33:59) يَاأَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ قُلْ لأَزْوَاجِكَ وَبَنَاتِكَ وَنِسَاءِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ يُدْنِينَ
عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ جَلابِيبِهِنَّ ذَلِكَ أَدْنَى أَنْ يُعْرَفْنَ فَلاَ يُؤْذَيْنَ Prophet, tell your wives, your
daughters and women of the believers to draw their garments
over them–this is more likely to make them recognisable and so
not be harassed.

أَذًى ʾadhan [n./v. n.] 1 disease, affliction, injury, ailment, to
trouble (2:196) فَمَنْ كَانَ مِنْكُمْ مَرِيضًا أَوْ بِهِ أَذًى مِّنْ رَأْسِهِ فَفِدْيَةٌ so if any of
you is ill, or has an ailment of the scalp, he should compensate 2
ill-treatment, offence, insult (3:186) وَلَتَسْمَعُنَّ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ مِنْ
قَبْلِكُمْ وَمِنَ الَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا أَذًى كَثِيرًا and you are sure to hear from those
who were given the scripture before you, and from those who
associate others with God, a lot of insults 3 little hurt, trifling
damage (3:111) لَنْ يَضُرُّوكُمْ إِلاَّ أَذًى they will never [be able to] inflict
on you but a trifling damage 4 hardship, inconvenience, to be
afflicted by affliction (4:102) وَلاَ جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِنْ كَانَ بِكُمْ أَذًى مِّنْ مَطَرٍ أَوْ
كُنْتُمْ مَرْضَى أَنْ تَضَعُوا أَسْلِحَتَكُمْ it is no sin for you to lay aside your arms
if you are afflicted by heavy rain or illness 5 painful condition,
impurity, soiling (2:222) وَيَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْمَحِيضِ قُلْ هُوَ أَذًى and they ask
you [Prophet] about menstruation, say, ‘Menstruation is an
impurity/painful condition.’

25

إرم
